author: namapane
body:
This PR includes a number of updates, clean-ups and minor improvements of the MF subsystems aimed at long-term maintenance and future development.

1. Uniformize the creation of interpolators between the DDD and DD4Hep geometry builders. Besides removing some code duplication, this enables both versions to read merged tables instead than one individual table for each volume, and opens the possibility of distributing only the merged tables in [cms-data/MagneticField-Interpolation](https://github.com/cms-data/MagneticField-Interpolation), dropping the individual tables. (Note: the DDD version is deprecated, but cannot be removed until the whole DDD support is dropped, starting from [here](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/blob/master/Configuration/StandardSequences/python/MagneticField_cff.py). It also turns out that the TEve-based MF visualiaztion only works with the DDD builder since it relies on TGeoMgrFromDdd, and no equivalent exists for DD4hep, to my knowledge).
Also, added the possibility to read tables for an absolute path, which can be useful for special studies (recent use case: special sets of tables for model version 170812 Run I, Run II, and MTCC-like conditions made available for comparison to proponents of the Z mass analysis on  `/eos/cms/store/group/alca_magfield/CMSSW/`)
1. Release the scripts to convert tables for the "final" MF model, version 170812, that can be released in the future.
1. Put some order in the different places where the MF XML geometry was defined, using standardized cff files. 
1. Handle an extremely rare case of numerical accuracy at the barrel-endcap boundary. Although this has practically zero impact on tracking (it was never noticed), it is better to solve it.
1.  Use the correct values for nominal magnet currents everywhere. The values currently in the code are a bit larger than in reality (e.g. 18268 instead of 18164). This derives from a misunderstanding at the time of MTCC: the currents used as input for early FEM models were tuned in an attempt to reproduce the central field strength, and for some reason these values were picked instead of the actual currents. (Subsequent improvements of the model result in excellent agreement with no need to tweak the currents). This fix has no effect on results since the values of nominal currents are only used to pick the map corresponding to the nominal working point, so the precise value that is used does not matter (i.e.: a current of 18000 or 18200 A equally gives the map for the nominal 3.8 T conditions). However, these shifted values in the code can cause some confusion among users (received some inquiries on this recently).

#### PR validation:
- repeated all validation steps indicated in the [MF documentation](https://twiki.cern.ch/twiki/bin/view/CMSPublic/SWGuideMagneticField?redirectedfrom=CMS.SWGuideMagneticField#Development_workflow)
- standard regression vs pre-computed values succeeds
- ad-hoc tests reading merged tables with the DDD builder, and of reading tables from absolute paths.
title: Updates and maintenance of the MagneticField subsystem
